evals.prompt: How do I set up HttpClient in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Add provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([...]), withFetch()) to app.config.ts providers. Inject with http = inject(HttpClient) in your service. Always type responses: http.get<User[]>('/api/users').

evals.prompt: How do I create a functional interceptor in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Use HttpInterceptorFn: const authInterceptor: HttpInterceptorFn = (req, next) => { const token = inject(AuthService).token(); return next(req.clone({ setHeaders: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } })); }. Register via withInterceptors([authInterceptor]).

evals.prompt: How do I use httpResource() for reactive HTTP calls?
evals.expected_output: Use httpResource<T>(() => `/api/users/${this.userId()}`) — it auto-refetches when the signal in the URL factory changes. Access states via .isLoading(), .hasValue(), .error(), .value(). Call .reload() to force refresh.

evals.prompt: Working on user.service.ts — how should I make API calls?
evals.expected_output: Encapsulate all HTTP calls in this service — never in components. Use httpResource() for reactive GET calls. Use http.post<T>() for mutations with explicit subscribe or lastValueFrom. Handle errors in the service or global interceptor.

evals.prompt: How do I handle HTTP errors globally in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Create a functional error interceptor: catch errors with catchError in the interceptor pipeline. Use HttpContext to pass metadata (e.g., skip error handling for certain endpoints). Display errors via a notification service, not in components.

evals.prompt: How do I use resource() for non-HTTP async operations in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Use resource<T, P>({ request: () => params(), loader: ({ request }) => someAsyncOp(request) }) for custom async operations. Provides the same .isLoading(), .value(), .error() lifecycle as httpResource() but for arbitrary async sources.

should_not_trigger: How do I make HTTP requests with fetch() in plain JavaScript?
How do I use Axios for HTTP calls in a React app?
